title: Design System: Life First Aid Courses

display.purpose: Hero h1 only. Authority + scale. Never above ~62px.

headline.purpose: Section h2 headings. Same family and weight as display, smaller scale.

title.purpose: Card and component headings (h3). Bricolage at 700.

body.purpose: All running prose. Public Sans. Max 65–75ch line length.

label.purpose: Kicker eyebrows, course codes, category tags, form labels. Uppercase, tracked. One per section maximum.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Appears on card hover (industry, course, review cards). Ink-derived, directional.

shadows.name: nav-scroll
shadows.purpose: Added to sticky nav via JS on scroll past 20px.

shadows.name: btn-primary-glow
shadows.purpose: Signal Red CTA button at rest. Reinforces the primary action color.

motion.name: transition-reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll-reveal transition for .reveal elements. NOTE: elements must be visible by default; this enhances, it does not gate visibility.

motion.name: nav-drawer
motion.purpose: Mobile nav drawer slide-in from right.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Highest-priority action. One per viewport fold maximum.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; font-family: 'Public Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 15px; padding: 14px 22px; border-radius: 10px; border: 2px solid transparent; cursor: pointer; min-height: 48px; background: #e03b30; color: #fff; box-shadow: 0 10px 24px -10px rgba(224,59,48,0.7); transition: background 0.18s, transform 0.18s; text-decoration: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #b22a21; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f0a92b; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Dark Button
components.description: Secondary action alongside a primary. Never competes with Signal Red.
components.css: .ds-btn-dark { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; font-family: 'Public Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 15px; padding: 14px 22px; border-radius: 10px; border: 2px solid transparent; cursor: pointer; min-height: 48px; background: #12222c; color: #fff; transition: background 0.18s, transform 0.18s; text-decoration: none; } .ds-btn-dark:hover { background: #1b3340; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-dark:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f0a92b; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Lowest emphasis. Tertiary action or alongside dark button.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; font-family: 'Public Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 15px; padding: 14px 22px; border-radius: 10px; border: 2px solid #d2ccbd; cursor: pointer; min-height: 48px; background: transparent; color: #12222c; transition: border-color 0.18s, background 0.18s; text-decoration: none; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #12222c; background: #fff; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f0a92b; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Kicker / Eyebrow
components.description: Branded section eyebrow. One per section maximum — not a pattern for every heading.
components.css: .ds-kicker { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'Public Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #0a5d50; } .ds-kicker::before { content: ''; width: 22px; height: 2px; background: #e03b30; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Read-only trust badge. Used in hero for delivery modes.
components.html: On-Site At Your Venue
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e0dccf; border-radius: 30px; padding: 7px 13px; font-family: 'Public Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #1b3340; }

components.name: Light Card
components.description: Standard content card. Flat at rest, shadow + teal border on hover.
components.html:
Childcare & Education

HLTAID012 certified staff for your compliance obligations.
components.css: .ds-card-light { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e0dccf; border-radius: 14px; padding: 24px; transition: border-color 0.2s, transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-card-light:hover { border-color: #0d7c6b; transform: translateY(-4px); box-shadow: 0 18px 50px -22px rgba(18,34,44,0.35); }

components.name: Dark Feature Card
components.description: Feature card on the dark Why section. No hover lift — the dark section is already the context.
components.html:
Audit-Ready Documentation

Certificates your auditors and insurers accept — every time.
components.css: .ds-card-dark { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.09); border-radius: 14px; padding: 24px; } .ds-feat-ic { width: 44px; height: 44px; border-radius: 10px; background: rgba(240,169,43,0.16); display: grid; place-items: center; margin-bottom: 14px; }

components.name: Course Code Badge
components.description: Accreditation unit code on course cards. Teal-soft background, teal-deep text.
components.css: .ds-course-code { font-family: 'Public Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #0a5d50; background: #e3f0ed; padding: 5px 10px; border-radius: 7px; align-self: flex-start; text-transform: uppercase; }

narrative.overview:
This is the design language of someone the region trusts by name. Not a national franchise or a government portal — a known, experienced local whose credibility comes from showing up and doing the work for over a decade. The visual system is calm and capable: dark, grounded ink tones anchor a layout that never shouts; the Signal Red appears only where action is required; the Valley Teal carries trust and accreditation; the Wattle Gold marks things worth believing. Nothing decorates for its own sake.

Density is moderate. The site must work for both a WHS coordinator scanning compliance language and a first-timer figuring out which course applies to them. Information is legible at a glance — course codes, duration, certification type — without requiring users to read to understand.
keyCharacteristics: Dark ink base with selective use of red (action), teal (trust), amber (credential)
Two-family type system: Bricolage Grotesque for authority, Public Sans for clarity
Tactile, confident components that reward hover without performing for attention
Flat at rest, shadow on hover — depth is earned, not ambient
Mobile-first, section-based scroll rhythm; each fold has one job

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Red appears on one primary action per viewport. If two red buttons share a screen, one of them should be dark or ghost. Scarcity is the mechanism.

rules.name: The Teal Trust Rule
rules.body: Valley Teal touches only elements that carry accreditation or verification meaning: course codes, RTO badges, checkmarks, certification language. Do not use it decoratively.

rules.name: The Weight Contract Rule
rules.body: Bricolage Grotesque is always 700 or 800. Never 400 or 500 for headings — the contrast between display weight and body weight is what creates hierarchy.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces rest flat. The shadow token appears only on hover state or for the single persistently-elevated hero element. If a card grid looks flat and lifeless at rest, that is correct — interaction reveals depth.

dos: Use Signal Red for one primary CTA per viewport fold
Use Valley Teal exclusively for accreditation and trust signals
Keep body text at 16px minimum, line-height 1.6, max 65–75ch
Keep text-wrap: balance on all h1–h3 elements
Ensure WCAG 2.1 AA contrast (≥4.5:1) for all body text
Provide prefers-reduced-motion alternatives for all animations
Use Bricolage Grotesque at 700 or 800 only for headings
Size all interactive elements to ≥44×44px touch targets
donts: Don't use a hospital-blue, clinical-white, or medical-sterile aesthetic
Don't use a faceless national-franchise corporate look
Don't use Wattle Gold for body text on light backgrounds — contrast fails WCAG AA
Don't apply the kicker eyebrow pattern to every section — one per page maximum
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe
Don't use numbered section markers outside genuinely sequential flows
Don't gate content behind reveal animation opacity — reveals must enhance visible content
Don't add a third font family
Don't use SaaS startup defaults: gradient hero cards, cream-on-cream restraint
